tool.name: dashing2 dist
tool.description: compare sketches or sequences into a distance/similarity matrix
tool.command:
#import re #if $input_mode.mode == 'raw': ## Build a file-of-files of symlinks named by element_identifier (see ## the sketch tool for the rationale). The inputs are raw sequences, ## which dashing2 sketches on the fly using the options below. mkdir -p '@FFILE_DIR@' && #for $element in $input_mode.reference_collection: #set $name = re.sub('[^A-Za-z0-9._-]', '_', str($element.element_identifier)) ln -s '$element' '@FFILE_DIR@/${name}' && echo '@FFILE_DIR@/${name}' >> 'ffile.txt' && #end for #set $use_query = $input_mode.query.use_query == 'yes' #if $use_query: #for $qelement in $input_mode.query.query_collection: #set $qname = re.sub('[^A-Za-z0-9._-]', '_', str($qelement.element_identifier)) ln -s '$qelement' '@FFILE_DIR@/${qname}' && echo '@FFILE_DIR@/${qname}' >> 'qfile.txt' && #end for #end if
dashing2 dist -p \${GALAXY_SLOTS:-8} -S${input_mode.sketch_params.sketch_size} $input_mode.alphabet $input_mode.sketch_mode #if str($input_mode.sketch_params.kmer_length) != '': -k${input_mode.sketch_params.kmer_length} #end if #if str($input_mode.sketch_params.window_size) != '': --window-size ${input_mode.sketch_params.window_size} #end if #if str($input_mode.sketch_params.spacing) != '': --spacing '${input_mode.sketch_params.spacing}' #end if $input_mode.sketch_params.no_canon $input_mode.sketch_params.long_kmers #if str($input_mode.sketch_params.seed) != '': --seed ${input_mode.sketch_params.seed} #end if $input_mode.parse_by_seq $measure #if not $use_query: $result_shape #end if #if $sparse.mode == 'topk': --topk ${sparse.topk} #elif $sparse.mode == 'threshold': --similarity-threshold ${sparse.threshold} #end if $binary_output #if $use_query: -Q 'qfile.txt' #end if --cmpout '$output' -F 'ffile.txt' #else: ## Pre-sketched input: dashing2 reads the stacked sketch from a single ## positional argument with --presketched, alongside its '.names.txt' ## sidecar (which it locates by appending '.names.txt' to the sketch ## path). dashing2 memory-maps the sketch read-write (it truncates the ## file in place even when the size is unchanged), so we must stage a ## WRITABLE copy: a symlink to the (read-only mounted) input dataset ## aborts with "Failed to truncate". 'cat >' yields a fresh writable ## file under a shared basename that also restores the original labels; ## without the names file the rows/columns are numbered instead. cat '$input_mode.sketch_bin' > presketched.bin && #if $input_mode.sketch_names: cat '$input_mode.sketch_names' > presketched.bin.names.txt && #end if dashing2 dist --presketched -p \${GALAXY_SLOTS:-8} $measure $result_shape #if $sparse.mode == 'topk': --topk ${sparse.topk} #elif $sparse.mode == 'threshold': --similarity-threshold ${sparse.threshold} #end if $binary_output --cmpout '$output' presketched.bin #end if

param.name: mode
param.label: Input type
param.help: Compare raw sequences (sketched on the fly) or a stacked sketch already produced by the dashing2 sketch tool. Pre-sketched comparison skips sketching entirely and is the way to re-use sketches across several comparisons.
param.option: Sequences—a FASTA/FASTQ collection, sketched on the fly
Pre-sketched—a stacked sketch from dashing2 sketch

param.name: reference_collection
param.label: Reference collection
param.help: A collection of raw FASTA/FASTQ datasets to compare. Two elements give a single pairwise distance; N elements give an all-pairs matrix.

param.name: use_query
param.label: Rectangular query-vs-reference comparison
param.help: Compare every reference dataset against a separate query collection, producing a rectangular |reference|×|query| matrix instead of an all-pairs matrix.

param.name: query_collection
param.label: Query collection
param.help: Datasets forming the query set. Each is compared against every dataset in the reference collection.

param.label: Parse each sequence separately
param.help: Treat every sequence within every raw input file as a separate entity rather than one entity per file. Only relevant when the inputs are raw sequences.

param.name: sketch_bin
param.label: Stacked sketch
param.help: The 'sketches' dataset emitted by dashing2 sketch (SetSketch modes). It is compared directly with --presketched, without re-reading any sequences.

param.name: sketch_names
param.label: Sketch names
param.help: The 'sketch names' (.names.txt) dataset emitted alongside the sketch. Optional, but supplying it restores the original row/column labels; without it the matrix is labelled with numeric indices.

tool.help:
dashing2 dist compares a set of inputs and emits a distance or similarity matrix. It is the comparison half of dashing2, offered here with two input modes.
.. class:: infomark
**Note:** ``dist`` and ``cmp`` are interchangeable aliases for this same sub-command in dashing2; they invoke identical behaviour. This tool is called ``dist`` for consistency, but any documentation that refers to ``dashing2 cmp`` also applies.
-----
Input *****
**Sequences mode** takes a **reference collection** of raw FASTA/FASTQ datasets, which are sketched on the fly using the sketch-construction options below. The element names become the row/column labels.
* **Two elements** yield a single pairwise distance. * **N elements** yield an all-pairs matrix. * Supplying a **query collection** yields a rectangular reference-vs-query matrix.
In sequences mode the **Sketch construction**, **Sketching mode** and **Sequence alphabet** options control how the inputs are sketched (identical to the *dashing2 sketch* tool).
**Pre-sketched mode** skips sketching entirely: supply the **sketches** dataset produced by *dashing2 sketch* (its stacked SetSketch output) and, to keep the original labels, the accompanying **sketch names** dataset. This is the way to re-use one set of sketches across several comparisons without re-reading the sequences each time. Pre-sketched comparison applies to the SetSketch modes (default and ``--full``); it does not apply to the exact ``--set`` / ``--countdict`` outputs. The comparison measure, output shape and sparse/binary options apply in both modes.
-----
Options *******
=========================== ========================== =========== =========================== **Option** **Flag** **Default** **Description** --------------------------- -------------------------- ----------- --------------------------- Comparison measure --distance / --containment similarity Quantity emitted per pair Output shape --asymmetric-all-pairs symmetric Dense matrix layout Sparse Top-k --topk off Nearest-neighbour graph Sparse threshold --similarity-threshold off Thresholded similarity graph Binary output --binary-output False float32 instead of text =========================== ========================== =========== ===========================
See the *dashing2 sketch* tool help for the full list of sketch-construction and sketching-mode parameters, which are shared between the two tools.
-----
Output ******
A single distance/similarity matrix. The symmetric all-pairs result is an upper-triangular PHYLIP matrix; square and rectangular results are flat tab-separated matrices; Top-k and threshold results are compressed-sparse-row (CSR) text. ``--binary-output`` replaces the text with a float32 binary matrix.
